# T11: Implied Relationship — Lawrence Draft Card

## Input

> I have a 1942 WWII draft registration card for Warren Dean
> Lawrence. The card names the registrant as Warren Dean Lawrence.
> In the contact/person-who-will-always-know-your-address field, it
> lists "Mrs. Warren Dean Lawrence." The card does not explicitly
> state her relationship to the registrant. How should I classify
> that relationship evidence?

## MUST Criteria

- [ ] Classify "Mrs. Warren Dean Lawrence" as evidence that implies
  a relationship, not as a relationship stated by the record
- [ ] State the inference explicitly, such as possible wife or other
  woman connected to a man named Warren Dean Lawrence
- [ ] Classify the relationship evidence as **Indirect** for a
  spouse/marital relationship question
- [ ] Identify evidence that could confirm or refute the inference,
  such as a marriage record, 1940 or 1950 census household, or birth
  certificate naming Warren Dean Lawrence's father
- [ ] Distinguish what the record states from what it suggests

## MUST NOT Criteria

- [ ] Must NOT state that Mrs. Warren Dean Lawrence was his wife as
  an established fact
- [ ] Must NOT treat courtesy title plus shared name as direct proof
  of marriage
- [ ] Must NOT omit the need for confirming or refuting evidence
- [ ] Must NOT use the phrases "primary source" or "secondary
  source"; correct use of Primary Information is allowed

## Ground Truth

The draft card is an original source for the recorded draft
registration details. The entry "Mrs. Warren Dean Lawrence" is
evidence that implies a relationship, but it does not state what the
relationship was. She may have been the registrant's wife, but if the
registrant's father shared the same name, she could instead have been
his mother. For a spouse or marital relationship question, this is
indirect evidence requiring correlation with confirming or refuting
records such as a marriage record, census household, or birth
certificate naming the father.
